disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic weight-bearing joint pain flared over 48 hours after an unusually long walk (mechanical overexertion)
trigger_question_examples: When did the pain get worse and was there anything that set it off?
What were you doing before it flared?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It has ached for years but it blew up over the last two days after I went for an unusually long walk.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is worse on weight-bearing/movement and relieved by rest, with short morning stiffness resolving within 30 minutes
trigger_question_examples: Is it worse when you move or when you rest?
How long does the morning stiffness last?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's worst when I try to stand or move; resting settles it. The stiffness in the morning eases off within about half an hour.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Absolute inability to weight-bear on the affected leg — acutely bedbound
trigger_question_examples: Can she stand or put any weight on it?
Is she able to walk to the bathroom?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She can't put any weight on it at all — she's stuck in bed and can't get to the toilet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Red-flag screen negative: no fever, no rigors, no systemic toxicity, and no prior hot/red/acutely swollen joint
trigger_question_examples: Has she had any fever, shivering or shaking chills?
Has the joint ever come up hot, red and suddenly swollen before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shakes at all, and she doesn't feel hot. It's never come up hot and red like an infection before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Existing analgesia is regular paracetamol with minimal effect; anti-inflammatory tablets avoided due to a distant gastritis history
trigger_question_examples: What has she taken for the pain and did it help?
Is there anything she can't take?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She takes her regular paracetamol but it's barely touching it. She stays off the anti-inflammatory tablets because of an old stomach problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High falls and toileting-access risk — no commode or frame at home, bathroom down a long hallway, spouse is the sole carer
trigger_question_examples: How is she managing to get to the toilet?
Is there any equipment or help at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's no commode or frame here and the toilet is right down the hall — I'm terrified she'll fall trying to get there.
